content: burrow destroy archive layout: <archiveRoot>/<burrowId>/{events.jsonl, messages.jsonl, runs.json} per SPEC §14.4. archiveRoot defaults to paths.archiveDir (= dataDir/archive). events.jsonl uses the same SPEC §14.1 envelope as live tail; messages.jsonl uses a sibling 'message' envelope; runs.json wraps {burrow, runs[], exportedAt} so consumers can grep via jq. Empty burrows still produce all three files (events.jsonl + messages.jsonl as zero-byte, runs.json as a JSON object with runs:[]).

title: destroyBurrowStorage stays separate from the destroy CLI command
rationale: SPEC §14.4 destroy is a 6-step flow: stop, export events, export messages, export runs, delete workspace, prune live rows + mark destroyed. Workspace teardown belongs to the provider (steps 1+5) and is wired in Phase 7 with the rest of the CLI surface. destroyBurrowStorage in src/events/destroy.ts owns only the storage half — archive then prune live rows then markDestroyed inside one call. Phase 7's CLI command will compose: providerStop → destroyBurrowStorage → providerRemoveWorkspace. Splitting this way keeps Phase 6 testable today without faking a provider.
